member.name: T:Refit.BodyAttribute
member.summary: Set a parameter to be sent as the HTTP request's body.
member.remarks:
There are four behaviors when sending a parameter as the request body:
- If the type is/implements , the content will be streamed via .
- If the type is , it will be used directly as the content unless [Body(BodySerializationMethod.Json)] is set which will send it as a .
- If the parameter has the attribute [Body(BodySerializationMethod.UrlEncoded)], the content will be URL-encoded.
- For all other types, the object will be serialized using the content serializer specified in the request's .

member.name: T:Refit.IApiResponse
member.summary: Base interface used to represent an API response.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.Headers
member.summary: Gets the HTTP response headers.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.ContentHeaders
member.summary: Gets the HTTP response content headers as defined in RFC 2616.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.IsSuccessStatusCode
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the request was successful.
member.remarks: A successful status code does not imply a response body. Per RFC 9110 a 204 (No Content), a 304 (Not Modified), a response to HEAD, or any 2xx with zero-length content carries no content, so this intentionally does not narrow . Use or when you need deserialized content.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.IsSuccessful
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the request was successful and there wasn't any other error (for example, during content deserialization).
member.remarks: "No error" is not "has content": a 2xx with a null or empty body deserializes to null without an error, so this does not narrow content. Use or when you need deserialized content.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.IsReceived
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether a response was received from the server.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.StatusCode
member.summary: Gets the HTTP response status code.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.ReasonPhrase
member.summary: Gets the reason phrase which typically is sent by the server together with the status code.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.RequestMessage
member.summary: Gets the HTTP Request message which led to this response.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.Version
member.summary: Gets the HTTP Message version.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse.Error
member.summary: Gets the exception object in case of unsuccessful request or response.
member.remarks: and methods can be used to check the type of error. An unsuccessful response is not guaranteed to carry an error (for example, a non-2xx response constructed without one), so a failed state does not narrow this to non-null. Null-check it, or use / for null-safe typed access.

member.name: M:Refit.IApiResponse.HasRequestError(Refit.ApiRequestException@)
member.summary: Checks if the call failed before a response was received from the server.
member.param: The object in case of an unsuccessful request.
member.returns: true if the call failed before a response was received from the server, otherwise false.

member.name: M:Refit.IApiResponse.HasResponseError(Refit.ApiException@)
member.summary: Checks if the call failed due to an unsuccessful response from the server.
member.param: The object in case of unsuccessful response.
member.returns: true if the call failed due to an unsuccessful response from the server, otherwise false.

member.name: T:Refit.IApiResponse`1
member.typeparam: The deserialized response content type.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse`1.Content
member.summary: Gets the deserialized request content as .

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse`1.HasContent
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether deserialized is available.
member.remarks: This is the covariance-safe way to null-check with flow analysis: if (response.HasContent) { /* response.Content is non-null here */ }. The base success/error/header members are intentionally not redeclared, so a single setup on the generic interface is observed through the non-generic as well.

member.name: P:Refit.IApiResponse`1.IsSuccessfulWithContent
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the request was successful and deserialized is available.
member.remarks: This restores single-check content narrowing — if (response.IsSuccessfulWithContent) { /* response.Content is non-null */ } — that can no longer promise (a successful response may carry no body). It is a brand-new generic-only member rather than a new shadow of a base member, so it references directly and behaves correctly through mocks and explicit interface implementations.

member.name: T:Refit.IRequestBuilder
member.summary: Defines a method for creating a delegate that executes a REST API method with the specified parameters and returns the result.
member.remarks: The returned delegate can be used to invoke a REST API method dynamically, given an HTTP client and an array of arguments. This interface is typically used by code generation or dynamic proxy libraries to construct method invokers at runtime. Implementations may use reflection and may require referenced types to be preserved when trimming assemblies.

member.name: P:Refit.IRequestBuilder.Settings
member.summary: Gets the settings used by this request builder.

member.name: M:Refit.IRequestBuilder.BuildRestResultFuncForMethod(System.String,System.Type[],System.Type[])
member.summary: Builds a delegate that executes the specified REST method using the provided HTTP client and arguments.
member.remarks: The returned delegate uses reflection to invoke the specified method and may require referenced interfaces and data transfer objects (DTOs) to be preserved when trimming assemblies. This method is typically used to dynamically generate REST API client calls at runtime.
member.param: The name of the interface method to generate the REST call delegate for. Must correspond to a method defined on the target interface.
An array of parameter types for the target method, or null to infer from the method signature. The order must match the method's parameter list if specified.
An array of generic argument types to use when constructing a generic method, or null if the method is not generic.
member.returns: A delegate that takes an HttpClient and an array of argument values, and returns the result of invoking the specified REST method. The return type matches the method's declared return type.

member.summary: An optional capability for an that can deserialize a response body incrementally into an . Refit uses this for interface methods that return , reading items as they arrive instead of buffering the whole response.

How the body is framed (a single JSON array or newline-delimited JSON).
member.returns: An asynchronous sequence of deserialized values.

member.name: T:Refit.ISynchronousContentDeserializer
member.summary: An optional capability for an that can deserialize an already-buffered response body string synchronously. Refit uses this for and , which let callers peek at an error body from inside an exception filter (catch (ApiException ex) when (ex.TryGetContentAs<Error>(out var error))), where awaiting is not allowed by the CLR.

member.name: M:Refit.ISynchronousContentDeserializer.DeserializeFromString``1(System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes synchronously into an object of type .
member.typeparam: The type to deserialize the content to.
member.returns: The deserialized object of type .

member.name: T:Refit.ISynchronousContentSerializer
member.summary: An optional capability for an that can serialize a request body synchronously (buffered or streamed). Refit uses this when is or , allowing the System.Text.Json source-generated fast-path to engage. The fast-path runs through the synchronous serialization primitives (SerializeToUtf8Bytes / Serialize(Utf8JsonWriter, ...)); the built-in SerializeAsync(Stream) used by JsonContent bypasses it and uses the metadata logic instead.

member.name: M:Refit.ISynchronousContentSerializer.ToHttpContentSynchronous``1(``0)
member.summary: Serializes synchronously into a buffered .
member.typeparam: The type of the object to serialize.
member.returns: A buffered (for example a ByteArrayContent) containing the serialized object.

member.name: M:Refit.ISynchronousContentSerializer.ToStreamingHttpContent``1(``0)
member.summary: Creates an that serializes through the synchronous fast-path onto the request stream, flushing asynchronously, without buffering the whole body. Best for large uploads.
member.typeparam: The type of the object to serialize.
member.returns: A streaming that writes the serialized object when sent.

member.name: F:Refit.RequestBodySerializationMode.Default
member.summary: The default: the body is serialized asynchronously (via JsonContent). This uses the metadata-based System.Text.Json logic, never the source-generated fast-path.

member.name: F:Refit.RequestBodySerializationMode.Buffered
member.summary: The body is serialized synchronously into a buffer and sent as a ByteArrayContent. This lets the source-generated fast-path engage (with fast-path-eligible options) and sets a Content-Length header. Best for small-to-medium bodies. Requires an .

member.name: F:Refit.RequestBodySerializationMode.Streamed
member.summary: The body is serialized through a Utf8JsonWriter written to the request stream and flushed asynchronously. This keeps the source-generated fast-path while bounding peak memory (the writer buffers in pooled chunks rather than materializing the whole body), at the cost of a Content-Length header. Best for large uploads. Requires an .

member.name: T:Refit.RequestBuilder
member.summary: Provides static methods for creating request builders for Refit interface types. This class enables the generation of strongly-typed HTTP client implementations based on interface definitions and optional settings.
member.remarks: Request builders generated by this class use reflection to analyze the provided interface type and create implementations for HTTP API calls. When using trimming or linking, ensure that referenced interfaces and data transfer objects are preserved, as reflection is required for correct operation. All members of this class are thread-safe and can be used concurrently across multiple threads.

member.name: F:Refit.RequestBuilder._platformRequestBuilderFactory
member.summary: The platform request builder factory used to create builders.

member.name: M:Refit.RequestBuilder.ForType``1(Refit.RefitSettings)
member.summary: Creates an HTTP request builder for the specified interface type.
member.remarks: Use this method to obtain a request builder for a given API interface, typically for advanced scenarios such as custom client generation or integration with dependency injection. The returned builder can be used to create HTTP requests corresponding to the methods defined on the interface.
member.typeparam: The interface type that defines the HTTP API methods to be implemented by the request builder. Must be an interface.
member.param: The Refit settings to use for configuring the request builder. May be null to use default settings.
member.returns: An instance of IRequestBuilder T that can be used to construct HTTP requests for the specified interface type.

member.name: M:Refit.RequestBuilder.ForType``1
member.summary: Creates a request builder for the specified type, enabling construction of requests against its members.
member.typeparam: The type for which to create the request builder. This type's methods will be available for request construction.
member.returns: An object that can be used to build requests for the specified type.

member.name: M:Refit.RequestBuilder.ForType(System.Type,Refit.RefitSettings)
member.summary: Creates an implementation of the specified Refit interface for making HTTP requests.
member.remarks: The returned IRequestBuilder uses reflection to analyze the provided interface type. When using trimming or linking, ensure that all referenced interfaces and data transfer objects are preserved to avoid runtime errors.
member.param: The interface type that defines the HTTP API contract. Must be a non-generic interface decorated with Refit attributes.
Optional settings to customize the behavior of the generated request builder. If null, default settings are used.
member.returns: An IRequestBuilder instance that can be used to construct HTTP requests for the specified interface.

member.name: M:Refit.RequestBuilder.ForType(System.Type)
member.summary: Creates an instance of an IRequestBuilder for the specified Refit interface type.
member.remarks: The specified interface type must be decorated with Refit attributes to define the API endpoints. If trimming is enabled, ensure that all referenced interfaces and data transfer objects are preserved, as reflection is used to analyze the interface methods.
member.param: The interface type that defines the Refit API contract. Must be a non-generic interface decorated with Refit attributes.
member.returns: An IRequestBuilder instance that can be used to construct HTTP requests for the specified interface type.

member.name: T:Refit.RequestBuilderFactory
member.summary: Default implementation of IRequestBuilderFactory.

member.summary: Reflection-based request builder that turns Refit interface calls into HTTP requests.

member.name: M:Refit.RestMethodInfoInternal.ParameterRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
{(([^/?\r\n])*?)}
Explanation:
○ Match '{'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Loop lazily any number of times.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [^\n\r/?].
○ Match '}'.

member.name: M:Refit.SystemTextJsonContentSerializer.GetFastPathJsonSerializerOptions
member.summary: Creates that are eligible for the System.Text.Json source-generated fast-path on serialization. Unlike , these options add no converters and do not set , both of which disable the fast-path. Assign a source-generated (a generated in or mode) to enable it. The fast-path runs through the synchronous serialization primitives (SerializeToUtf8Bytes / Serialize(Utf8JsonWriter, ...)); only the built-in SerializeAsync(Stream) (used by JsonContent) bypasses it for the metadata logic.
member.returns: Fast-path-eligible .

member.name: T:Refit.UrlResolutionMode
member.summary: Defines how Refit combines the client's base address with a method's relative URL.

member.name: F:Refit.UrlResolutionMode.RefitLegacy
member.summary: The historical Refit behavior: relative paths must start with /, the base address path is prepended, and a trailing slash on the base address is trimmed to avoid a double slash. This is the default.

member.name: F:Refit.UrlResolutionMode.Rfc3986
member.summary: RFC 3986 / resolution: the relative URL is merged with the base address using rules. A relative path without a leading slash is appended to the base address path, while a leading slash replaces it. The leading-slash requirement is relaxed in this mode.
